households.source: ONS, Families and households in the UK, 2025 bulletin

population.source: ONS, Population estimates for the UK, mid-2024

annual_demand_twh.description: UK electricity final consumption by end users (TWh/year), used for the per-MWh-delivered framing. Total demand including losses was 320.2 TWh in 2025; the lower final-consumption figure is used so the per-MWh framing is not overstated by network losses.
annual_demand_twh.source: DESNZ Energy Trends, March 2026 statistical release (2025 annual data, section 5 / table 5.2)

deflator.source: ONS, CPIH Index 00 All Items 2015=100, annual averages (series L522)

bill.source: DESNZ, Digest of UK Energy Statistics (DUKES) 1.3, Sales of electricity and gas by sector

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of direct UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 316,000 NHS nurses, in today's money.
factoids.label: NHS nurses' annual salaries paid by one year of direct renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: NHS Employers, Agenda for Change pay scales 2026/27

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 559,000 social homes — land included — in today's money.
factoids.label: social homes (land included) the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Cebr for Shelter and the National Housing Federation (2024)

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 4 Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations in the UK, in today's money.
factoids.label: Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: New Civil Engineer, reporting EDF's 2025 annual results (20 February 2026)

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 4,400 large new secondary schools, in today's money.
factoids.label: large new secondary schools the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: DfE, School Rebuilding Programme — actual scheme costs for new large secondary schools

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 110 of the largest new NHS hospitals, in today's money.
factoids.label: the largest new NHS hospitals the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Update on the New Hospital Programme (2026) — largest schemes about £2bn each

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 53 Queen Elizabeth-class aircraft carriers, in today's money.
factoids.label: Queen Elizabeth-class aircraft carriers the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Delivering Carrier Strike (2017) — £6.212bn for both ships

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 10 Elizabeth Lines — London's Crossrail — in today's money.
factoids.label: Crossrail / Elizabeth Lines the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: National Audit Office, Crossrail — a progress update (2021); £18.9bn cost estimate

factoids.frame: the current £220bn+ full cost would have built
factoids.sentence: The current £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables would have built 220 billion-pound football stadiums, in today's money.
factoids.label: billion-pound football stadiums the current £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Tottenham Hotspur Stadium reported build cost (about £1.2bn, 2019-20)

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 241,000 experienced schoolteachers, in today's money.
factoids.label: experienced schoolteachers' annual salaries paid by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: STPCD 2025, Upper Pay Range UPS3, England excluding London (2025-26)

factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy pays
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 245,000 police officers, in today's money.
factoids.label: police officers' annual salaries paid by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Police Federation of England & Wales, constable pay scales (top of scale, from 1 September 2025)

factoids.figure_label: years of free school meals for every UK schoolchild
factoids.frame: a year of this subsidy could fund
factoids.sentence: A year of UK renewable subsidy could fund free school meals for every UK schoolchild for more than 2 years, in today's money.
factoids.label: years of free school meals for every UK schoolchild, funded by one year of UK renewable subsidy, in today's money
factoids.source_name: DfE free school meals funding (£505/pupil/year); UK pupil population ~10.67m (DfE, StatsWales, Scottish Government, DENI 2024-25)
